CommonLifecycleModel.description:
Lifecycle phases for common models.
Maps phase names to phase configurations.
Phase names follow pattern: {command}_{action}_{suffix}
Examples: config_clear, config_fetch, deploy_plan_before, deploy_apply_after
CommonLifecycleModel.title: CommonLifecycleModel

CommonLifecyclePhaseModel.description: Model for common lifecycle phase with script validation.
CommonLifecyclePhaseModel.title: CommonLifecyclePhaseModel

DeploymentApprovalModel.description:
Deployment-level approval metadata.

Presence of this block signals that approvals are declared for this deployment.
An empty ``approvers`` dict is silently treated as no gate.
The CLI emits this metadata for audit; enforcement is done by the CI/CD system.

approvers.description: Named approver entries. Key is a short identifier used as a cross-reference from stage overrides.
approvers.title: Approvers

DeploymentApprovalModel.title: DeploymentApprovalModel

DeploymentStageApprovalModel.description: Per-stage approval override: restricts which spec-level approvers apply to this stage.

approvers.description: List of approver keys from spec.approvals.approvers that apply to this stage
approvers.title: Approvers

DeploymentStageApprovalModel.title: DeploymentStageApprovalModel

DeploymentStageModel.description:
Model for a deployment stage (pipeline execution step).

Stages enable:
- Sequential multi-step execution (e.g., provision → configure → verify)
- Execution ordering and dependency management (DAG execution via depends_on)
- Failure handling per stage
- Semantic provisioner selection via type with explicit override options

Provisioner Selection Priority:
1. Explicit 'provisioner' field (highest priority)
2. Explicit 'topology' field (topology defines provisioner)
3. Convention from 'type' field (auto-select based on stage type)
4. ERROR if ambiguous (multiple provisioners match type)

Stages do NOT define environments or approvals - those are deployment-level.
Stages do NOT affect artifact paths - use deployment.spec.deployment for layering.

name.description: Unique stage name - pipeline step identifier (e.g., 'provision', 'configure', 'verify')
name.title: Name

type.description: Stage type - determines provisioner selection strategy and execution semantics
type.title: Type

provisioner.description: Explicit provisioner name from workspace (required if multiple provisioners match type)
provisioner.title: Provisioner

topology.description: Explicit topology name from workspace (alternative to provisioner - topology defines provisioner and resources)
topology.title: Topology

scripts.description: Additional scripts to execute for this stage (extends provisioner behavior)

depends_on.description: List of stage names this stage depends on (enables DAG execution)
depends_on.title: Depends On

on_failure.description: Action to take on stage failure: 'stop' halts pipeline, 'rollback' reverts, 'continue' proceeds
on_failure.title: On Failure

health_checks.description: Health checks to run against this stage after provisioning.
health_checks.title: Health Checks

approval.description: Per-stage approval override: list of approver keys from spec.approvals.approvers. Absent means no stage-level restriction — spec-level approvers apply as-is.

DeploymentStageModel.title: DeploymentStageModel

FeatureStoreModel.description:
Model for feature flag definitions.

Feature flags can be sourced from various backends using registered integrations.
The 'store' field specifies the integration type, and the 'value' field
specifies the feature flag identifier within that store.

key.description: Feature flag key name for referencing in configurations
key.title: Key

store.description: Feature store type: constant, environment, or azure-appconfig

value.description: Feature flag identifier: literal value for constant, env var name for environment, flag name/key for integration stores
value.title: Value

version.description: Optional version for store-based feature flags (supported by some integrations)
version.title: Version

FeatureStoreModel.title: FeatureStoreModel

FeatureStoreType.description:
Enumeration of supported feature flag store backend types.

Store types map to integration types registered in IntegrationFactory:
- CONSTANT/ENVIRONMENT: Built-in resolvers (no integration required)
- AZURE_APPCONFIG: "azure-appconfig" integration type

Can be expanded to include more feature flag services:
- LaunchDarkly, Split, Unleash, etc.
FeatureStoreType.title: FeatureStoreType

FirewallDefaultsModel.description:
Model for a default firewall rule (direction, permission, comment).
Used to set baseline allow/deny behavior for inbound/outbound traffic.

direction.description: Direction of traffic for the default rule: 'in' or 'out'.

permission.description: Permission for the default rule: 'allow' or 'deny'.

comment.description: Optional comment or documentation for the default rule.
comment.title: Comment

FirewallDefaultsModel.title: FirewallDefaultsModel

FirewallDirection.description: Firewall rule direction: inbound (in) or outbound (out).
FirewallDirection.title: FirewallDirection

FirewallPermission.description: Firewall rule permission: allow or deny.
FirewallPermission.title: FirewallPermission

FirewallProtocol.description: Firewall rule protocol: tcp, udp, or icmp.
FirewallProtocol.title: FirewallProtocol

FirewallRuleModel.description:
Model for an individual firewall rule.
Supports direction, protocol, port(s), interface, source/destination, and comments.
Validates IP/CIDR, port formats, and interface names.

direction.description: Direction of traffic: 'in' for inbound, 'out' for outbound.

proto.description: Protocol for the rule: 'tcp', 'udp', or 'icmp'. Optional.

anyOf.description: Single port (int), port range (str like '80:90'), or list of ports/ranges. Optional.

from.description: Source IP address or CIDR (for inbound rules). Optional.
from.title: From

to.description: Destination IP address or CIDR (for outbound rules). Optional.
to.title: To

comment.description: Optional comment or documentation for the rule.
comment.title: Comment

FirewallRuleModel.title: FirewallRuleModel

HealthCheckModel.description:
A single health check applied to a deployment stage after provisioning.

Two check types:
- ``http``  — HTTP(S) GET; passes if response code matches ``expect_status``.
- ``tcp``   — TCP connect to ``host:port``; passes if connection succeeds.

Both types also support checking Terraform output values:
- ``output_key`` — name of a Terraform output whose value should be used as
  the URL (http) or ``host:port`` (tcp).  Takes precedence over ``url`` /
  ``host`` + ``port`` when present.

Examples (YAML)::

    health_checks:
      - name: api-endpoint
        type: http
        output_key: api_url
        expect_status: 200
        timeout: 10
      - name: db-port
        type: tcp
        host: 10.0.0.5
        port: 5432

name.description: Unique check name within the stage
name.title: Name

url.description: URL to GET (http type). Overridden by output_key.
url.title: Url

host.description: Hostname or IP (tcp type). Overridden by output_key.
host.title: Host

output_key.description: Terraform output key whose value provides the URL or host:port target.
output_key.title: Output Key

HealthCheckModel.title: HealthCheckModel

ResourceDependencyModel.description:
Capability-based resource dependency model.

Resources declare what TYPE/CATEGORY of resource they need,
allowing the workspace to resolve to specific resource instances.
This enables resource reusability across different workspaces and providers.

Examples:
    - category: "networking", subcategory: "virtual_network"
      → Satisfied by Azure VNet, AWS VPC, or GCP VPC
    - category: "database", subcategory: "nosql"
      → Satisfied by Cosmos DB, DynamoDB, or Firestore
    - category: "networking", subcategory: "private_dns"
      → Satisfied by Azure Private DNS, Route53, or Cloud DNS

category.description: Resource category required (e.g., networking, compute, database, storage, security)
category.title: Category

subcategory.description: Optional subcategory for more specific requirements (e.g., virtual_network, private_dns, nosql, blob)
subcategory.title: Subcategory

resource_type.description: Optional specific resource type for even more granular matching (e.g., cosmosdb_account, storage_account)
resource_type.title: Resource Type

description.description: Human-readable explanation of why this dependency exists
description.title: Description

optional.description: If True, workspace can deploy without this dependency
optional.title: Optional

ResourceDependencyModel.title: ResourceDependencyModel

SourceModel.description:
Reusable model for source configuration that references a repository.

Used by resources, modules, and other components to specify where their
source artifacts come from and where they should be deployed/built.

Example usage in a resource:
    source:
      repository: my-infra-repo
      source_path: terraform/modules/vpc
      target_path: build/vpc

repository.title: Repository

source_path.title: Source Path

target_path.description: Target path where artifacts should be built/deployed (relative to build/deploy directory)
target_path.title: Target Path

description:
Root model for the generated platform configuration artifact.

This is the complete output structure written as workspace.json (or similar)
and consumed by IaC tools and builders.  It is assembled by the builder layer
from the individual service-loaded input models (workspace, providers, resources,
modules, namespaces, firewalls, …) plus the deployment overlay.

meta.description: Metadata about the platform artifact (name, annotations, labels, tags)

spec.description: Full specification including workspace, providers, provisioners, resources, namespaces, modules, firewalls, deployment settings.

title: PlatformArtifactModel
